description:
fz-diag render snippet today writes the source line verbatim regardless
of the use_color flag (src/diag/render.rs:186-ish). Color is applied
only to the underline glyph below.

A nicer presentation would inline-wrap the spanned bytes in red/cyan
ANSI codes so the diagnostic visually highlights the problem region
within the source line itself, not just under it. Example:

  10 |     let f = foo(bar(baz));
     |                 ^^^^^^^^

vs.

  10 |     let f = foo([RED]bar(baz)[RESET]);
     |                 ^^^^^^^^

## Scope

- Compute (start_col, end_col) for the spanned bytes within
  expanded_line (already computed at line 209-213 in render.rs for the
  underline).
- When use_color is set, split expanded_line into three parts:
  pre-span, spanned, post-span. Wrap the spanned part in color_pre /
  color_post (already declared at line 219-221 for the underline).
- Care with the byte-to-col mapping for multi-byte UTF-8 / tab-expanded
  text — reuse the existing byte_to_col machinery.

## Acceptance

- Visual: a diagnostic with use_color shows the spanned region inline-
  colored as well as underlined.
- No regression in non-color mode (e.g., piped output).
- Golden diagnostics tests still pass (no ANSI escapes leak into
  non-color paths).

## Out of scope

Multi-line spans, span-truncation for very wide lines.
title: diag: inline-color spanned bytes within source line snippet
